content:
1 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Everyone is talking about Amazon’s additional $13B investment in AI infrastructure in India.
I think they’re talking about the wrong thing.
The real story isn’t the infrastructure.
It’s what businesses do because of it.
When cloud providers invest billions, compute becomes more accessible, AI capabilities improve, and the barrier to experimentation continues to fall.
Which means the competitive advantage is shifting.
It won’t come from having access to AI.
It will come from answering questions like:
• Which workflows should we automate first?
• Is our data actually AI-ready?
• How do we govern AI responsibly?
• Where will AI create measurable ROI instead of more complexity?
We’re entering a phase where AI is no longer a technology project.
It’s becoming a business transformation project.
The companies that start building their AI operating model today will be the ones setting the pace over the next few years.
Infrastructure is becoming a commodity.
Execution is becoming the differentiator.
What do you think will be the biggest obstacle to enterprise AI adoption over the next 24 months?
